what_it_does: Renders a fixed 1600x900 presentation-style HTML slide with a centered headline, two embossed stat boxes, a horizontal six-metric stat band, and an SVG brand-geometry diagram connecting two engines into a hub.
observations: Uses Plus Jakarta Sans from Google Fonts and a warm white, gold, ochre, bronze, charcoal palette defined in CSS variables.
The main canvas is `.view`, fixed at 1600px by 900px and centered on a gray page background.
Top content shows the title "Proven, and built to scale." followed by embossed boxes for "6 yrs" and "$1.5M+".
A diagonal-hatched stat band lists six metrics: live hubs, clients, sales, users, saw gains, and recommend.
The lower SVG uses a hexagon for Engine one, a diamond with a V for Engine two, dashed connector paths, and a hub icon.
Includes subtle background plan-grid texture, bottom hatch footer, and a circular V mark.
standouts: Layered presentation composition with stats, diagram, and brand marks in one static canvas.
Embossed metric boxes contrast with the flatter hatched stat band.
The SVG diagram uses geometry and dashed connectors to express a two-engine system feeding a hub.
conventions: Single-file static HTML with all styling in a `<style>` block.
Heavy use of absolute positioning for slide layout control.
CSS custom properties define the palette, while some SVG fills and strokes repeat literal color values.
Pseudo-elements provide decorative hatch and band border layers.
No JavaScript and no external assets beyond the Google Fonts stylesheet.
concerns: Fixed 1600x900 sizing and absolute positioning make the layout tied to one slide dimension.
Several CSS selectors for `.cap`, `.capL`, and `.capR` are defined but not used in the markup.
Some palette variables appear unused because SVG elements repeat hard-coded color values.
notes: This file is a static branded proof-and-scale slide combining headline metrics with a two-engine system diagram.
questions: Are the Engine one and Engine two labels placeholders or final product names?
Should the unused caption styles correspond to removed side captions?
Are the metric labels and values final content or presentation mock data?
purpose_vs_actual: The title and content indicate a proof-and-scale presentation slide, and the file implements that as a static visual composition.
intention_signals: It appears intended to communicate maturity, investment, adoption, and a two-part system architecture through restrained brand visuals and quantified proof points.
novelty: Combines embossed top-level proof stats, a hatched metric band, and a geometric engine-to-hub diagram inside one static slide composition.
contribution: Contributes a stat-focused presentation substrate with embossed proof metrics, a horizontal traction band, and geometric two-engine-to-hub visual language that a fused system could retain as a metrics-and-architecture layout pattern.
summary_for_embedding: A static 1600x900 branded HTML slide showing proof metrics, a six-item stat band, and an SVG two-engine system diagram connected to a hub.
intention_for_embedding: This was for presenting product maturity and scale through quantified traction and a simple branded system-architecture metaphor.
